title: iris tenax var. thompsonii
intro: Iris tenax var. thompsonii, also known as Western blue iris, is a subspecies of the Iris tenax plant, belonging to the iris family and native to North America. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it beneficial in treating wounds and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps. Iris tenax var. thompsonii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
